16 scenes per Part — plenty of room for variation. Scenes are part of the Part, not the Pattern.
merlin_tips: Merlin's scene model: Scene B is on the RIGHT. In the buildup example, he loads his buildup scene to slot B and slowly fades right for increasing intensity.
Scene stacking: Scenes 9-16 go on the RIGHT (Scene B). After fading to Scene B, load the next scene to slot A and fade back left.
The scene stacking sequence: Start → fade right to Scene 9 → load Scene 2 to A → fade left → load Scene 10 to B → fade right → etc. This creates a natural progression through scenes.
manual.workflow_context: Press [SCENE B]+[TRIG] to assign a scene to slot B. Merlin's buildup technique: load a 'tension' scene to slot B (delay feedback up, filter resonance up, distortion up, reverb up), then slowly crossfade right for a gradual buildup.
common_mistakes: Building scenes that are too extreme — Merlin's approach is gradual, stacking small changes for natural progression
Forgetting to mute Scene B when switching to a new pattern with its own Part — the new Part loads its own scene assignments

manual.primary: Interpolates between the parameter values of Scene A (leftmost) and Scene B (rightmost). Any crossfader position between the extremes blends the two scenes proportionally.
manual.func_combo: N/A — physical slider, no FUNC combination.
manual.menu_path: N/A — physical control. Crossfader position shown at bottom-right of the screen.
manual.leds: No LED. Position indicated on screen.
tips: Scene locks override parameter locks — the crossfader always takes priority.
If two empty scenes are assigned, the crossfader does nothing.
The crossfader center position has a natural volume dip — use XLV/XVOL for equal-energy crossfading.
The crossfader can control ANY lockable parameter across ALL 8 tracks simultaneously.
The MKII crossfader is upgraded with a smoother, silkier action compared to the MKI.
manual.note: Center position has volume dip — use XVOL for equal-energy crossfading
merlin_tips: Merlin identifies 3 key things you can do: (1) Fade from DEFAULT to a scene, (2) Fade from SCENE to SCENE, (3) Fade from SCENE back to DEFAULT.

His metaphor: 'A scene allows you to undertake an expedition from the base camp (default settings) into new territory (a scene) and move further as you keep loading new scenes. Returning to base camp is always possible by muting a scene and fading to it.'
Muting a scene while the crossfader is there causes an instant jump — no fade. Use this for dramatic drops.
For live inputs, XDIR in scenes lets the crossfader control input volume — effectively giving you 10 channels of crossfader control (8 tracks + 2 inputs).
Use XVOL (AMP page) and XLEV (track level) in scenes for pre/post-FX volume control via crossfader. XVOL lets reverb tails die naturally.
Scene stacking creates a linear journey through your performance: each new scene builds on the previous one.
manual.workflow_context: The crossfader interpolates between Scene A (left) and Scene B (right). Merlin's performance flow: start with crossfader left (default or Scene A), slowly fade right toward Scene B for buildup, then either snap back (mute scene) or load a new scene and continue fading. XVOL and XLEV give you pre/post-FX volume control through scenes.
common_mistakes: Center position has a volume dip — use XVOL for equal-energy crossfading
Trying to use the crossfader without setting up scenes first — it does nothing without scene assignments
Not understanding that scene locks override p-locks — the crossfader ALWAYS wins over step-level parameter locks

manual.primary: Multi-purpose key. In Grid Recording: places/removes trigs on step 1. With [BANK]: selects Bank A. With [PTN]: selects Pattern 1. With [SCENE A/B]: assigns Scene 1. In Tracks mode (no recording): trigs Track 1 if disconnected.
manual.func_combo: [FUNC] + [TRIG 1] = Place lock trig on step 1 (Grid Recording) / One-shot trig toggle.
all_combos: [TRIG 1] = Place/remove trig on step 1 (Grid Rec) / Trig disconnected Track 1 (Tracks mode)
[BANK] + [TRIG 1] = Select Bank A
[PTN] + [TRIG 1] = Select Pattern 1 in current bank
[SCENE A/B] + [TRIG 1] = Assign Scene 1 to slot A or B
[FUNC] + [TRIG 1] = Place lock trig / Toggle one-shot (Grid Rec)
[TRIG 1] + encoder turn = Parameter lock for step 1
[TRIG 1] + [UP/DOWN] = Sample Lock menu (Grid Rec)
[TRIG 1] + [LEFT/RIGHT] = Micro Timing menu (Grid Rec)
[TRIG 1] + [YES] = Preview trig on main outputs
[TRIG 1] + [CUE] = Preview trig on cue outputs
[TRIG 1] + [RECORD] = Copy trig
[TRIG 1] + [PLAY] = Clear trig locks
[TRIG 1] + [STOP] = Paste trig
[TRIG 1] + [NO] = Convert trig type (sample→trigless→lock cycle)
manual.leds: Red = sample trig. Flashing red = sample trig with p-lock or sample lock. Green (bright) = trigless trig. Green (dim) = lock trig. Yellow = one-shot trig. Yellow/red alternating = fired one-shot. Unlit = no trig.
tips: In LIVE RECORDING mode, [TRIG 9-16] input sample trigs on tracks 1-8 (not [TRIG 1-8]).
In Chromatic mode, TRIG keys become a mini-keyboard. Yellow = C notes, red = white keys, unlit = black keys.
In Quick Mute mode, [TRIG 1-8] mute audio tracks, [TRIG 9-16] mute MIDI tracks.
Hold a trig and turn LEVEL knob to open the Sample Lock browser.
merlin_tips: Merlin's sample lock revelation: 'By using sample locks, I can play any sample from the sample list at any step on any track that contains a flex machine in any pattern in any bank.' The trig keys + LEVEL encoder are the gateway to this.
Hold a trig + turn LEVEL encoder = sample lock browser. Select any sample from the slot list for that step.
Hold a trig + lock the START parameter = slice lock. Select which slice plays on that step.
The math: 128 slots x 64 slices = 8,192 available sounds per sample list. With Static list too, that's 16,384 total.
One-shot trigs ([FUNC]+[TRIG]) are essential for recorder triggers — they fire once and don't repeat, preventing overwrite on pattern loop.
Merlin's pattern variation technique: same Part, different trigger patterns. Strip triggers for a break, add extra triggers + locks for a buildup.
manual.workflow_context: In Grid Recording mode, press [TRIG] to place/remove triggers. Hold [TRIG]+[UP/DOWN] for sample locks. Hold [TRIG]+turn LEVEL for sample lock via encoder. P-lock parameters by holding [TRIG] and turning any encoder. [FUNC]+[TRIG] places a one-shot or lock trig.
common_mistakes: Not understanding that a trig key's LED color tells you what's on it: steady red = normal trig, flashing red = sample lock
Placing triggers without being in Grid Recording mode
Forgetting that [FUNC]+[TRIG] places special trig types (one-shot, lock trigs) — not regular trigs

manual.primary: Selects the active pattern page when GRID RECORDING mode is active. Patterns can have 1-4 pages (16-64 steps). Also used in Slots/Slices trig modes to navigate sample slot/slice pages.
manual.func_combo: [FUNC] + [PAGE] = Opens the SCALE SETUP menu where pattern/track length, time signature, and tempo multiplier are set.
all_combos: [PAGE] = Switch pattern page (in Grid Recording) / Navigate slot/slice pages (in Slots/Slices mode)
[FUNC] + [PAGE] = Open Scale Setup menu
[PAGE] + [RECORD] = Copy active pattern page
[PAGE] + [PLAY] = Clear active pattern page
[PAGE] + [STOP] = Paste to active pattern page
[PAGE] (in Chromatic mode) = Acts as an additional keyboard key
[PAGE] (in Scale Setup) = Quick-change total pattern/track length
[UP] + [PAGE] = Activate FILL mode (hold for duration)
[DOWN] + [PAGE] = Activate FILL mode for one pattern cycle
[UP] + [DOWN] + [PAGE] = Latch FILL mode
manual.menu_path: SCALE SETUP menu (via FUNC+PAGE) → NORMAL mode: Pattern Scale (steps/total), Tempo Multiplier. PER TRACK mode: Scale Track, Tempo Multiplier, Master Length, Master Scale.
parameters: NORMAL: PATTERN SCALE (1-64 steps / 16,32,48,64 total), TEMPO MULTIPLIER (1/8X to 2X)
PER TRACK: SCALE TRACK (per-track length), TEMPO MULTIPLIER (per-track), MASTER LENGTH (overall pattern restart), MASTER SCALE (overall time signature)
manual.leds: Four <PAGE> LEDs above the [PAGE] key indicate which page is active/playing. For 16-step patterns, only LED 1:4 is lit and [PAGE] has no effect.
tips: Trigs are auto-copied when extending pattern length (e.g., extending from 16 to 32 steps copies page 1 to page 2). Disable in Personalize.
In Scale Setup, press [TRIG] keys to quickly set the number of steps; press [PAGE] to quickly set total length.
FILL mode ([UP/DOWN]+[PAGE]) is essential for drum fills and conditional trig patterns.
2X tempo multiplier doubles the step resolution to 32nd notes — great for detailed hi-hat patterns.
merlin_tips: Merlin's examples all use 16-step (1-page) patterns, but the principles extend to multi-page patterns for longer sequences.
manual.workflow_context: Press [PAGE] to cycle through pattern pages (up to 4 pages = 64 steps). [FUNC]+[PAGE] opens Scale Setup for changing track length and time signature. 4 LEDs show which pages have content.
common_mistakes: Not realizing patterns can be longer than 16 steps — press PAGE to access steps 17-32, 33-48, 49-64
Forgetting that each track can have a DIFFERENT length via Scale Setup — creating polyrhythms

sequences.description: Place hi-hats on all even steps (8th notes)
Set a sample lock: REC → hold TRIG → UP/DOWN → YES
Set up timestretch: Audio Editor → Attributes → Playback Setup

sample_lock.description: Sample lock active on this step
